description: Defines CSS architecture rules for shared layout, reusable components, page-specific styling, and future theming.

rules.name: Prefer shared CSS for reusable UI patterns
rules.description: Reusable layout, tables, forms, buttons, panels, cards, spacing, and theme primitives should live in shared CSS files.

rules.name: Page CSS is only for page-specific styling
rules.description: Page-level CSS should only contain styling that is genuinely unique to that page.

rules.name: Avoid duplicated page overrides
rules.description: Repeated page-level overrides should be moved into shared CSS or design-system files.

rules.name: Use content-driven protected layouts where appropriate
rules.description: Protected desktop content panels should generally be content-driven in width unless there is a clear layout reason to stretch.

rules.name: Avoid inline styles
rules.description: Inline styles should be avoided except for dynamic runtime-calculated styling that cannot reasonably live in CSS classes.
